questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Chalk and Talk method?
questions.explanation: The Chalk and Talk method involves the teacher writing on a chalkboard and discussing the content with the students. It is a traditional teaching method that requires a lecture-style delivery, but it can also be accompanied by visual aids to enhance understanding.

questions.question: What is a common disadvantage of using PowerPoint presentations in the classroom?
questions.explanation: PowerPoint presentations can sometimes lead to a lack of engagement among students, as they can be too focused on text and not enough on discussion or interaction.

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of using the Chalk and Talk method?
questions.explanation: The Chalk and Talk method can encourage critical thinking, as students are required to listen and respond to the teacher's questions. It also allows for group discussion and note-taking.

questions.question: What is a common feature of PowerPoint presentations?
options: C) Slides with text and images
questions.explanation: PowerPoint presentations typically include slides with text and images, which can be used to convey information and engage students.

questions.question: How can teachers adapt the Chalk and Talk method to engage students?
options: A) By using more visual aids
questions.explanation: Teachers can adapt the Chalk and Talk method by incorporating more discussion and using more visual aids to engage students and make the content more accessible.
